title: Lethedon oblonga
uses: Lethedon oblonga has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported antimalarial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have analgesic and antipyretic effects, making it a potential treatment for fever and pain. In some cultures, Lethedon oblonga is used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. However, there is limited scientific evidence to support these claims, and further research is needed to confirm its medicinal uses. More studies are required to fully understand the potential health benefits and risks associated with using Lethedon oblonga.
taxonomy: Lethedon oblonga is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. The Celastraceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the staff vine family. This family consists of around 60-100 genera and over 1,000 species. The plants within the Celastraceae family are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. They are characterized by their simple, alternate leaves and often woody stems.
